question: Ranthambor national park is situated in
question_latex: Ranthambor national park is situated in
solution: Ranthambor national park is situated in Rajasthan.

question: One of the most important functions of botanical gardens is that [MHT CET 2008]
question_latex: One of the most important functions of botanical gardens is that [MHT CET 2008]
options: A. One can observe tropical plants there
B. They allow ex situ conservation of germplasm
C. They provide the natural habitat for wild life
D. They provide a beautiful area for recreation
answer: B. They allow ex situ conservation of germplasm
solution: Botanical gardens are collection of various types of living plants. Ex situ conservation means conservation of plants or animals in the artificial habitats, which are quite similar to the normal habitats of these organisms. In this way, botanical gardens provide ex situ conservation of germplasm.

question: Which one of the following heavy metals in water is responsible to cause growth retardation and bone deformation in humans?
question_latex: Which one of the following heavy metals in water is responsible to cause growth retardation and bone deformation in humans?
solution: Cadmium causes growth retardation and bone deformation in humans. Arsenic causes disturbances in blood circulation and nervous disturbances. Lead causes lead poisoning. Mercury also causes poisoning.

question: Ozone depletion from the stratosphere has become reason for causing which of the following diseases? [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Ozone depletion from the stratosphere has become reason for causing which of the following diseases? [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Ozone depletion from the stratosphere has become reason for causing Skin Cancer.

question: After completing his civil engineering degree, Ramesh was appointed as trainee in the R and D city development office. He was to work on the project of laying water repellent roads in the city. Which one of the following method will he decide to implement for sustainability?
question_latex: After completing his civil engineering degree, Ramesh was appointed as trainee in the R and D city development office. He was to work on the project of laying water repellent roads in the city. Which one of the following method will he decide to implement for sustainability?
options: A. Mixing wax in the cement to be used.
B. Adding plastic powder in the cement to be used.
C. Mixing 'Polyblend' in bitumen to be used.
D. Adding cement in the mixture to be used.
answer: C. Mixing 'Polyblend' in bitumen to be used.
solution: Polyblend is type of polymer made up of plastic. It helps in sustainable use of plastics for road and construction activities with environmental and economic advantages. Blends of polyblend when used to lay roads enhance the bitumen's water repellent properties and helped to increase the road life. Ahmed Khan was first to use it. He is founder of K.K. Plastic waste management Pvt. Ltd.

question: Fluoride pollution mainly affects
question_latex: Fluoride pollution mainly affects
solution: Prolonged intake of fluoride polluted water causes stiffening of bone and joints particularly spinal cord. Due to affinity with calcium, fluoride stores in bones which causes mottling of teeth, bone pains and outward bending of legs from the knees. This is known as Knock Knee Syndrome.

question: SO2 pollution is indicated by
question_latex: $SO_2$ pollution is indicated by
solution: Lichens (Usnea) are the indicator of air pollution, as these are very sensitive to air pollution (particularly $SO_2$ pollution).

question: A lake with nutrients is called
question_latex: A lake with nutrients is called
solution: A lake highly enriched with nutrients is called eutrophic.

question: Match Column I with Column-II and select the correct option.
question_latex:
Match Column I with Column-II and select the correct option.

Column I: (i) High levels of Sulphur dioxide, (ii) Endosulfan, (iii) Pollen, (iv) Gaseous and particulate lead
Column II: (a) Natural pollutant, (b) Agricultural practices, (c) Automobile exhaust, (d) Electric power plants
solution: Pollen grains released in large numbers also act as natural pollutants. Endosulfan is an agricultural pesticide used in large quantity causing pollution. Gaseous and particulate lead cause various health hazardous effects to man and other animals and plants. High levels of sulphur dioxide cause air pollution causing ill effects on human health.

question: Which one of the following is used to reduce industrial exhaust emissions to the atmosphere to control air pollution? [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Which one of the following is used to reduce industrial exhaust emissions to the atmosphere to control air pollution? [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Catalytic converter helps to reduce exhaust emission to atmosphere to control air pollution. It uses catalyst conducting redox reaction to reduce the harmful emissions.

question: Megadiversity countries of the globe are ___ in number. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Megadiversity countries of the globe are ___ in number. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: One of the 12 megadiversity countries of the globe, India has 2.4% of total land area of the world. We have identified around 45000 plant species and nearly double the number of animal varieties from our natural wealth.

question: Which of the following is not shortwave radiation?
question_latex: Which of the following is not shortwave radiation?
solution: Radio waves are not short wave radiations. These have high wavelength, i.e., $10^3$ m.

question: Manas sanctuary is located at
question_latex: Manas sanctuary is located at
solution: Manas Wildlife Sanctuary is situated at Kamrup (Asom). It covers 80 sq km area. Its key vertebrate species are tiger, wild boar, sambhar, golden langoor, one-horned rhino, swamp deer, wild dog and wild buffalo.

question: Which one of the following is NOT caused by global warming? [MHT-CET 2018]
question_latex: Which one of the following is NOT caused by global warming? [MHT-CET 2018]
solution: Increase in crop production is not caused by global warming. Global warming causes melting of glaciers, changes in rainfall patterns, and increase in sea level.

question: Which of the following is NOT a source of air pollutant?
question_latex: Which of the following is NOT a source of air pollutant?
options: B. Use of CNG as fuel
answer: B. Use of CNG as fuel
solution: Use of CNG as fuel will help to reduce air pollution as it does not release pollutants in atmosphere. In Delhi most of the public transport vehicles are running with CNG as a measure of air pollution control.

question: The species listed in Red Data Book are
question_latex: The species listed in Red Data Book are
solution: Those species whose population has been greatly reduced or whose natural habitats have been disturbed due to which these are near the extinction are grouped under the category of threatened species. IUCN maintains a Red Data Book which contains a record of threatened species. These include vulnerable, endangered and rare species.

question: One of the ex situ conservation method for endangered species is
question_latex: One of the ex situ conservation method for endangered species is
solution: Ex situ conservation is the preservation of components of biological diversity outside their natural habitat. It includes cryopreservation, off site collections, gene banks and tissue culture. In situ conservation is the preservation of biological diversity in their natural wild conditions, usually in the form of biosphere reserves, national parks and wild life sanctuaries.

question: Global agreement in specific control strategies to reduce the release of ozone depleting substances was adopted by
question_latex: Global agreement in specific control strategies to reduce the release of ozone depleting substances was adopted by
solution: The Montreal protocol on substances that deplete the ozone layer is a landmark international agreement designed to protect the stratospheric ozone layer. The treaty was originally signed in 1987 (effected in 1989) and substantially amended in 1990 and 1992. The Montreal protocol stipulates that the production and consumption of compounds that deplete ozone in the stratosphere — $CFC_s$, halons, carbon tetrachloride and methyl chloroform — are to be phased out by 2000 (2005 for methyl chloroform).

question: Common indicator organism of water pollution is
question_latex: Common indicator organism of water pollution is
solution: Since large populations of Escherichia coli are found in human colon, the presence of E. coli in water indicates that it has been contaminated with faecal matter. Thus, E. coli is commonly known as indicator of water pollution.

question: Ozone depletion in stratosphere shall result in
question_latex: Ozone depletion in stratosphere shall result in
options: D. Increased incidence of skin cancer
answer: D. Increased incidence of skin cancer
solution: Ozone depletion in stratosphere shall result in increased incidence of skin cancer and cataract.

question: Particulate pollution from industries and power generation plants can be controlled by [MHT-CET 2019]
question_latex: Particulate pollution from industries and power generation plants can be controlled by [MHT-CET 2019]
solution: Electrostatic precipitators are used to control particulate pollution from industries and power generation plants.

question: Fly ash is a/an
question_latex: Fly ash is a/an
options: C. New name of orchid plant
D. Causal organism of various disease
solution: Fly ash is a light airborne particulate matter. Fly ash is mainly produced by coal based thermal plants. It should be removed through wet method and used in building material. Insectivorous plants are plants which use insects for $N_2$ requirement, e.g., Drosera. Orchid plants are epiphytic, which grow on other plants for support only.

question: Which element is the cause of itai-itai disease?
question_latex: Which element is the cause of itai-itai disease?
solution: Cadmium (Cd) poisoning leads to itai-itai (ouch-ouch) disease. Cadmium consumption causes diarrhea, bone deformation, kidney damage, retarded growth, CNS injury etc. Mercury (Hg) poisoning causes Minamata disease, lead (Pb) consumption causes damage to liver, heart, kidney and reduction in haemoglobin formation, while black-foot disease is caused by chronic exposure to arsenic.

question: The oxygen concentration at the floor of the deep ponds and lakes is very low because of the [MHT CET 2007]
question_latex: The oxygen concentration at the floor of the deep ponds and lakes is very low because of the [MHT CET 2007]
options: D. Large number of annual inhabitants
solution: The oxygen concentration at the floor of the deep ponds and lakes is very low because of the lesser amount of sunlight.

question: CFCs are responsible for
question_latex: $CFCs$ are responsible for
solution: $CFC_s$ (Chlorofluorocarbons) are mainly responsible for ozone layer depletion. $CFC_s$ are used as cooling materials in refrigerators and air conditioners, propel aerosol sprays, etc. UV ray breaks $CFC$ molecules and release chlorine molecules, which reduce the ozone content in the atmosphere. One chlorine free radical is sufficient to destroy a lac of ozone molecules.

question: Green house effect is due to the increased concentration of
question_latex: Green house effect is due to the increased concentration of
solution: Due to increase in $CO_2$ concentration, a thick layer of $CO_2$ is formed, which functions as glass panel of a green house that prevents the heat from being re-radiated out. This is called green house effect.

question:
Identify the sacred groves found in India from the following list. Choose the correct option given below. [MHT-CET 2023]
(i) Khasi and Jaintia hills in Meghalaya.
(ii) Western ghat region of Maharashtra.
(iii) Aravalli Hills of Rajasthan.
(iv) Chanda and Sarguja areas of Madhya Pradesh.
(v) Ladakh region of Himalaya.
question_latex:
Identify the sacred groves found in India from the following list. Choose the correct option given below. [MHT-CET 2023]
(i) Khasi and Jaintia hills in Meghalaya.
(ii) Western ghat region of Maharashtra.
(iii) Aravalli Hills of Rajasthan.
(iv) Chanda and Sarguja areas of Madhya Pradesh.
(v) Ladakh region of Himalaya.
solution: This is called in situ conservation. This is the most appropriate method of conservation.

question: In 1984, Bhopal gas tragedy was caused due to leakage of [MHT-CET 2014]
question_latex: In 1984, Bhopal gas tragedy was caused due to leakage of [MHT-CET 2014]
solution: In 1984, Bhopal gas tragedy was caused due to leakage of Methyl isocyanate.

question: Total number of all species of organisms in a given region is known as the region's
question_latex: Total number of all species of organisms in a given region is known as the region's
solution: Biota is the total number of all species of organisms in a given region. Flora is the plant species of a region while fauna is the animal species in an area.

question: Which one of the following is also called 'Terror of Bengal'? [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Which one of the following is also called 'Terror of Bengal'? [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Eicchornia crassipes is an alien species which grows tremendously causing threat to native species and can be one of the causes of Biodiversity loss.

question: Ozone (O3) depletion is due to
question_latex: Ozone ($O_3$) depletion is due to
solution: The main cause of ozone layer depletion is chlorofluorocarbons ($CFCs$) released from aerosol spray cans, polyurethane foams, air conditioners and refrigerators.

question: Carbon dioxide is called green house gas because it is
question_latex: Carbon dioxide is called green house gas because it is
options: A. Used in green house to increase plant growth
B. Transparent to heat but traps sunlight
C. Transparent to sunlight but traps heat
D. Transparent to both sunlight and heat
answer: C. Transparent to sunlight but traps heat
solution: The excess amount of $CO_2$ forms a thick blanket in the atmosphere which is transparent to sunlight but absorbs infra-red radiation trapping heat near the earth's surface. In this way, due to $CO_2$ blanket, the earth's atmosphere works very much like a green house which causes warming up of the interior. So, carbon dioxide is called green house gas.

question: Which one of the following is a natural cause of extinction of flora and fauna?
question_latex: Which one of the following is a natural cause of extinction of flora and fauna?
solution: The natural cause of habitat destruction is forest fire which destroys flora and fauna of that region completely.

question: The animal, extincted from India is
question_latex: The animal, extincted from India is
solution: The cheetah (Acinonyx jubatus) is a member of cat family. Cheetah have been known to exist in India for a very long time. But due to hunting and other purposes, cheetah in India became extinct before the twentieth century.

question: It is used in refrigerator and air conditioners and it is a source of Cl-
question_latex: It is used in refrigerator and air conditioners and it is a source of $Cl^-$
solution: Freon and other chlorofluorocarbon ($CFC$) compounds are used in refrigerators, air conditioners and as filling agent in aerosol, also cause pollution. CFCs do not degrade easily in the troposphere due to which they rise into the stratosphere, where they are broken by UV light. These are mainly responsible for ozone depletion.

question: All are sources of particulate pollutants EXCEPT _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: All are sources of particulate pollutants EXCEPT \underline{\hspace{1cm}} [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Nitrogen dioxide ($NO_2$) and nitrogen monoxide ($NO$) are released by automobiles and chemical industries as waste gases. These are gaseous pollutants, not particulate pollutants.

question: SO2 pollution affects
question_latex: $SO_2$ pollution affects
solution: At cellular level, $SO_2$ pollution destroys all membrane systems. In intense exposure to $SO_2$, there is bleaching of leaf pigments due to conversion of chl.-a to phaeophytin-a. Thus, $SO_2$ exposure has an impact on plant productivity. $SO_2$ pollution is the main cause of acid rain, which is threatening the shining of Taj Mahal. Mosses and lichens are very sensitive (indicator) to $SO_2$ pollution.

question: Which one of the following is the correct percentage of the two (out of the total of four) green house gases that contribute to the total global warming?
question_latex: Which one of the following is the correct percentage of the two (out of the total of four) green house gases that contribute to the total global warming?
solution: The various green house gases are $CO_2$ (warming effect 60%), $CH_4$ (20%), $CFC_s$ (14%) and nitrous oxide $N_2O$ (6%).

question: Which of the following is exhaustible but limited source of energy?
question_latex: Which of the following is exhaustible but limited source of energy?
solution: Exhaustible resources are natural resources with finite supply, which if used indiscriminately are likely to diminish and then get exhausted. Fossil fuel is a non-renewable (limited) exhaustible source of energy.

question: Out of total incident solar radiation, which type of radiations are absorbed by plants and photo synthetic bacteria? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Out of total incident solar radiation, which type of radiations are absorbed by plants and photo synthetic bacteria? [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The type of radiations absorbed by plants and photosynthetic bacteria is Photo synthetically active radiation (PAR).

question: Which one of the following is a major pollutant of automobile gases?
question_latex: Which one of the following is a major pollutant of automobile gases?
solution: 80% of automobiles exhaust is carbon monoxide. It is a colorless, odourless gas. When inhaled, this gas combines with blood haemoglobin about 200 times faster than does oxygen and results in oxygen deficiency.

question: Which of the following is non-biodegradable?
question_latex: Which of the following is non-biodegradable?
solution: The materials and poisons such as aluminium ions, mercurial salts and DDT that either do not degrade or degrade only extremely slowly in the natural environment are called non-biodegradable pollutants.

question: A taxon, which is facing an extremely high risk of extinction in the wild in immediate future is known as
question_latex: A taxon, which is facing an extremely high risk of extinction in the wild in immediate future is known as
solution: A taxon is critically endangered when it is facing an extremely high risk of extinction in the wild in the near future.

question: Which of the following strategies is not a correct approach to reduce global warming?
question_latex: Which of the following strategies is not a correct approach to reduce global warming?
options: A. Reducing the green-house gas emission by limiting the use of fossil fuels
B. Increase the vegetation cover particularly the forest for photosynthetic utilization of CO2
C. Minimising the use of nitrogen fertilizers in agriculture for reducing NO2 emission
D. Increasing the use of air conditioners, refrigeration unit and production of plastic
answer: D. Increasing the use of air conditioners, refrigeration unit and production of plastic
solution: Global warming is the warming/heating up of the earth's atmosphere due to depletion of ozone in the stratosphere. Major pollutants responsible for this depletion are chlorofluoro carbons ($CFCs$), nitrogen oxides and hydrocarbons. $CFCs$ are widely used as coolants in air conditioners and refrigerators, cleaning solvents, aerosol propellants and in foam insulation. So, decreasing the use of air conditioners, jet planes, green house gases, etc, or developing substitutes for $CFCs$ can reduce global warming.

question: Biodiversity Act of India was passed by the Parliament in the year
question_latex: Biodiversity Act of India was passed by the Parliament in the year
solution: Biodiversity Act of India was passed by the Parliament in the year 2002.

question: The total number of biodiversity hot spots in the world are
question_latex: The total number of biodiversity hot spots in the world are
solution: A biodiversity hotspot is a biogeographic region with a significant reservoir of biodiversity that is threatened with destruction. Initially, 25 biodiversity hotspots were identified but subsequently nine more have been added to the list bringing the total number of biodiversity hotspots in the world to 34.

question: Acid rain is mainly caused due to increase in the levels of the gas(es)
question_latex: Acid rain is mainly caused due to increase in the levels of the gas(es)
solution: Acid rain problem can be attributed mainly to atmospheric pollutants such as oxides of sulphur and nitrogen. The oxides of sulphur are released from the smoke stacks of coal fired power plants, smelters and other industries. The oxides of nitrogen came from combustion of fuels in automobiles as well as in power plants.

question: Rivet popper Hypothesis was given by ______ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Rivet popper Hypothesis was given by \underline{\hspace{1cm}} [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Rivet popper Hypothesis was given by Paul Ehrlich, an ecologist from Stanford, who gave an analogy to explain significance of diversity.

question: Industrial effluents rich in phosphorus causes overgrowth of algae. This is known as [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Industrial effluents rich in phosphorus causes overgrowth of algae. This is known as [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Sudden influx of phosphorus in the form of agricultural runoff or industrial effluents rich in phosphate content leads to eutrophication in water bodies causing overgrowth of algae.

question: Which one of the following is NOT an example of ex-situ conservation of endangered species? [MHT-CET 2017]
question_latex: Which one of the following is NOT an example of ex-situ conservation of endangered species? [MHT-CET 2017]
solution: National park is an example of in-situ conservation, not ex-situ conservation. Ex-situ conservation includes zoological parks, botanical gardens, culture collections, gene banks and cryopreservation.

question: Species richness is ______ at lower latitudes and there is steady ______ towards the poles. [2022]
question_latex: Species richness is \underline{\hspace{1cm}} at lower latitudes and there is steady \underline{\hspace{1cm}} towards the poles. [2022]
solution: Species richness is high at lower latitudes and there is a steady decline towards the poles.

question:
Which of the following are exotic herbs that affect the growth of native species?
(I) Rauwolfia Serpentina, (II) Parthenium sps, (III) Gnetum ula, (IV) Lantana camara
question_latex:
Which of the following are exotic herbs that affect the growth of native species?
(I) Rauwolfia Serpentina, (II) Parthenium sps, (III) Gnetum ula, (IV) Lantana camara
solution: Exotic species can threaten native species by outcompeting them for resources. Some exotic species in India includes Lantana, Parthenium, Siam weed, Mexican devil etc.

question: Paul Ehrlich suggested Rivet Popper Hypothesis in which he compared the ecosystem and species to ______ and ______ respectively. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Paul Ehrlich suggested Rivet Popper Hypothesis in which he compared the ecosystem and species to \underline{\hspace{1cm}} and \underline{\hspace{1cm}} respectively. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Paul Ehrlich suggested Rivet Popper Hypothesis in which he compared the ecosystem and species to airplane and rivets respectively to explain significance of diversity. Loss of species may not pose threat to the ecosystem only initially. Loss of key species will certainly cause threat in very short span of time. It will affect food chains, food web, energy flow, natural cycles, etc.

question: Which one of the following is INCORRECT with respect to Verhulst-Pearl Logistic Growth? [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Which one of the following is INCORRECT with respect to Verhulst-Pearl Logistic Growth? [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. It shows geometric growth in population
C. It is a sigmoid curve
D. It occurs when resources are limited
answer: A. It shows geometric growth in population
solution: A population growing in a habitat with limited resources shows initially a lag phase, followed by phases of acceleration and deceleration and finally an asymptote, when the population density reaches the carrying capacity. It shows logistic growth, not geometric growth.

question: The thickness of ozone in a column of air from the ground to the top of the atmosphere is measured in terms of
question_latex: The thickness of ozone in a column of air from the ground to the top of the atmosphere is measured in terms of
solution: The thickness of the ozone in a column of air from the ground to the top of the atmosphere is measured in terms of Dobson Units (DU).

question: Conservation in natural habitat is
question_latex: Conservation in natural habitat is
solution: In situ conservation is the conservation of living resources through their maintenance within the natural ecosystems in which they occur. In situ conservation includes a comprehensive system of protected areas such as the national parks, sanctuaries, natural reserves, biosphere reserves, etc.

question: Which one of the following is NOT the in-situ conservation of biodiversity? [MHT-CET 2018]
question_latex: Which one of the following is NOT the in-situ conservation of biodiversity? [MHT-CET 2018]
solution: Zoological parks are an example of ex-situ conservation. In-situ conservation includes wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and biosphere reserves.

question: World Summit on Sustainable Development (2002) was held in
question_latex: World Summit on Sustainable Development (2002) was held in
solution: In 1992, world leaders convened an Earth Summit in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Another Earth Summit was convened from 26th August to 4th September 2002 in Johannesburg, South Africa.

question: The cause of decline in the population of reptiles and birds is
question_latex: The cause of decline in the population of reptiles and birds is
solution: The reptiles and birds are mostly secondary or tertiary consumers. The concentration of DDT is increased in them. DDT is non-biodegradable pollutant, responsible for decline in the population of birds and reptiles.

question: The behavioural response to cope with the hot weather in their environment is very well exhibited by ..[2022]
question_latex: The behavioural response to cope with the hot weather in their environment is very well exhibited by ..[2022]
solution: Lizard exhibits behavioural response to cope with hot weather by moving to shaded areas or adjusting their body orientation.

question: It has been observed that species richness is very low in north and south poles. Which one of the following could be a possible reason for it? [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: It has been observed that species richness is very low in north and south poles. Which one of the following could be a possible reason for it? [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. Periodic glaciations like drastic climate changes.
B. Lesser drastic climatic changes throughout the year.
C. Availability of plenty of sunlight.
D. Lesser migrations have reduced gene flow and favored speciation.
answer: A. Periodic glaciations like drastic climate changes.
solution: Species richness or diversity for plants and animals decreases as we move away from equator to the poles. It is maximum in tropical rain forests. Species richness is high at lower latitudes and there is a steady decline towards the poles due to periodic glaciations and drastic climate changes.

question: Approval of Utilization of any biological resources for commercial or industrial purpose at local level is taken from [2022]
question_latex: Approval of Utilization of any biological resources for commercial or industrial purpose at local level is taken from [2022]
solution: Biodiversity Management Committees operate at the local level and are responsible for approving utilization of biological resources for commercial or industrial purposes.

question:
Select the correct option with respect to in situ type of conservation of biodiversity from the following. [MHT-CET 2023]
I) Botanical garden
II) National park
III) Seed bank
IV) Zoological park
V) Sacred groves
VI) Wildlife sanctuaries
VII) Biosphere reserve
question_latex:
Select the correct option with respect to in situ type of conservation of biodiversity from the following. [MHT-CET 2023]
I) Botanical garden
II) National park
III) Seed bank
IV) Zoological park
V) Sacred groves
VI) Wildlife sanctuaries
VII) Biosphere reserve
solution: In situ conservation of biodiversity involves conserving living organisms in their natural habitat. National park (II), Sacred groves (V), Wildlife sanctuaries (VI) and Biosphere reserves (VII) are all examples of in situ conservation.

question: Supreme Court of India has banned loudspeakers at public gatherings after ___ [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Supreme Court of India has banned loudspeakers at public gatherings after \underline{\hspace{1cm}} [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Supreme Court of India has banned loudspeakers at public gatherings after 10 pm. Laws which prohibit blowing horn in the areas of schools and hospitals need to be implemented strictly to curb decibel levels.

question: Match the scientist in column I with their contribution in column II and choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex:
Match the scientist in column I with their contribution in column II and choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2024]

Column I: (a) Alexander Van Humboldt, (b) Robert May, (c) David Tillman, (d) Paul Ehrlich
Column II: (i) Estimated 7 million species round the globe, (ii) Rivet popper hypothesis, (iii) Species Area Relationship, (iv) Productivity stability hypothesis
solution: Alexander Van Humboldt - Species Area Relationship; Robert May - Estimated 7 million species round the globe; David Tillman - Productivity stability hypothesis; Paul Ehrlich - Rivet popper hypothesis.

question: Biosphere reserves differ from national parks and wild life sanctuaries because in the former
question_latex: Biosphere reserves differ from national parks and wild life sanctuaries because in the former
options: A. Human beings are not allowed to enter
B. People are an integral part of the system
C. Plants are paid greater attention than the animals
D. Living organisms are brought from all over the world and preserved for posterity
answer: B. People are an integral part of the system
solution: In the biosphere reserve, people are an integral part, but not in National Parks and wild life sanctuaries.

question: Psilotum nudum is a/an ______ species.
question_latex: Psilotum nudum is a/an \underline{\hspace{1cm}} species.
solution: Psilotum nudum, also known as whisk fern, is an endangered species and is endemic to Sheshachalam Hills, Andhra Pradesh.

question: Tiger is not resident in which one of the following national park?
question_latex: Tiger is not resident in which one of the following national park?
solution: Gir National Park (Gujarat) is not concerned with tiger. The animals found in Gir national park are Asiatic lion, panther, striped hyaena, sambar, nilgai, cheetal, four-horned antelope and chinkara. Ranthambhor National Park, Sunderbans and Jim Corbett National Park (Uttarakhand) are tiger reserves.

question: During the tertiary treatment of sewage, chlorination is done to ____. [2022]
question_latex: During the tertiary treatment of sewage, chlorination is done to \underline{\hspace{1cm}}. [2022]
solution: During tertiary treatment of sewage, chlorination is done to kill pathogenic bacteria.

question: Which of the following is not used for disinfection of drinking water?
question_latex: Which of the following is not used for disinfection of drinking water?
solution: Phenyl is not used for disinfection of drinking water.

question: Chlorofluorocarbon is responsible for ______ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Chlorofluorocarbon is responsible for \underline{\hspace{1cm}} [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Chlorofluorocarbon is responsible for ozone depletion. CFCs move upwards and reach stratosphere. UV rays act on them and release Cl atoms. Cl degrades ozone releasing molecular oxygen.

question: Kyoto protocol has specified the commitments of different countries
question_latex: Kyoto protocol has specified the commitments of different countries
options: C. To prepare a world climate programme
solution: Kyoto protocol has specified the commitments of different countries to mitigate climate changes.

question: Identify the correct matched pair. [MHT CET 2008]
question_latex: Identify the correct matched pair. [MHT CET 2008]
options: D. Rann of Kutch - Wild ass
answer: D. Rann of Kutch - Wild ass
solution: The Rann of Kutch is the correct matched pair with Wild ass. Kaziranga is famous for one-horned rhino, Gir for Asiatic lion, and Corbett for tigers.

question: Nitrogen oxides produced from the emission of automobiles and power plants are the source of fine air borne particles which lead to
question_latex: Nitrogen oxides produced from the emission of automobiles and power plants are the source of fine air borne particles which lead to
solution: Depending on climate conditions, tiny particles of nitrogen and sulphur oxides may be airborne for a while and then fall to earth as dry acid deposition. Most of sulphur and nitrogen dioxides dissolve in atmospheric water to form weak solutions of $H_2SO_4$ and $HNO_3$. Winds can distribute them over great distances before they fall to earth in rain — this is called wet acid deposition.

question: Chipko movement is related to
question_latex: Chipko movement is related to
solution: In 1973 the Chipko movement was launched by Chandi Prasad Bhatt and Sundar Lal Bahuguna against large scale felling of trees by timber contractors in Uttaranchal hills.

question: Following are the advantages of using CNG as a fuel EXCEPT ______ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Following are the advantages of using CNG as a fuel EXCEPT \underline{\hspace{1cm}} [MHT-CET 2023]
options: B. It is adulteration proof fuel
C. It will never get exhausted
answer: C. It will never get exhausted
solution: CNG is advantageous over other fuels because it is economic, burns efficiently and is adulteration proof fuel but it may get exhausted.

question:
Given below are two statements. [2022]
Statement I: Availability of intense sunlight, warmer temperature and higher annual rainfall in tropics are responsible for species richness of fruit yielding trees.
Statement II: The variety of frugivorous organisms is more in tropics as compared to the temperate regions.
In the light of above statements, choose the most appropriate answer.
question_latex:
Given below are two statements. [2022]
Statement I: Availability of intense sunlight, warmer temperature and higher annual rainfall in tropics are responsible for species richness of fruit yielding trees.
Statement II: The variety of frugivorous organisms is more in tropics as compared to the temperate regions.
In the light of above statements, choose the most appropriate answer.
options: A. Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
B. Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
C. Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
D. Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
answer: A. Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
solution: Both statements are correct. Availability of intense sunlight, warmer temperature and higher annual rainfall in tropics favor high species richness. The variety of frugivorous organisms is also more in tropics.

question: Ban on use, sale, distribution and storage of plastic was implemented by the Maharashtra government in ______ year. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Ban on use, sale, distribution and storage of plastic was implemented by the Maharashtra government in \underline{\hspace{1cm}} year. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Ban on use, sale, distribution and storage of plastic was implemented by the Maharashtra government in 2018.

question: Polyblend, a fine powder of recycled modified plastic, was used _____
question_latex: Polyblend, a fine powder of recycled modified plastic, was used \underline{\hspace{1cm}}
solution: Polyblend, a form of recycled plastic, was used to lay roads. First attempted by Ahmed Khan as a recycling method.

question:
Given below are two statements: [MHT-CET 2024]
Statement-I: The particles with size 1.0 nm to 2.5 nm remains suspended in the air are known as particulate pollutants.
Statement-II: Particulate pollutants damage lungs by going deep into it.
In the light of above statements, choose the most appropriate answer.
question_latex:
Given below are two statements: [MHT-CET 2024]
Statement-I: The particles with size 1.0 nm to 2.5 nm remains suspended in the air are known as particulate pollutants.
Statement-II: Particulate pollutants damage lungs by going deep into it.
In the light of above statements, choose the most appropriate answer.
options: A. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
B. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
C. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
D. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
answer: B. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
solution: Both statement I and statement II are correct with respect to size of particles and their effect on human body.

question: Green house effect with respect to global climate refers to
question_latex: Green house effect with respect to global climate refers to
solution: Green house effect involves the heating (warming) up of earth's surface due to increasing amount of $CO_2$ in the atmosphere as its thick layer prevents the solar heat from being reradiated out of the earth's surface.

question: Kyoto protocol is related with
question_latex: Kyoto protocol is related with
solution: An international conference was held in Kyoto, the ancient capital of Japan on 1 to 10 December, 1997 of G-77 countries. It is popularly known as Kyoto protocol. In this, emphasis is given on global warming. It is the result of increasing use of green house gases such as $CO_2$, methane, oxides of nitrogen, $CFC_s$, etc.

question: Match the following in Column-I with their number found in India given in Column-II and choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex:
Match the following in Column-I with their number found in India given in Column-II and choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2024]

Column I: (a) Varieties of mango, (b) Biosphere reserves, (c) National parks, (d) Wildlife sanctuaries
Column II: (i) 14, (ii) 448, (iii) 2000, (iv) 90
solution: Varieties of mango = 2000, Biosphere reserves = 14, National parks = 90, Wildlife sanctuaries = 448.

question: With reference to given Pie-chart, identify the correct statement. [2022]
question_latex: With reference to given Pie-chart, identify the correct statement. [2022]
options: A. The species diversity of monocots is equal to the species diversity of dicots.
B. Flowering plants are the dominant group of plants showing highest species diversity.
C. Diversity of conifers is equal to the sum of diversity of ferns and mosses.
D. Diversity of dicot species is equal to the sum of ferns and moss species diversity.
answer: B. Flowering plants are the dominant group of plants showing highest species diversity.
solution: From the pie chart, flowering plants (both monocots and dicots) together represent the dominant group of plants showing the highest species diversity.

question: Which insecticide is more hazardous to human health?
question_latex: Which insecticide is more hazardous to human health?
solution: DDT is the most hazardous, non-biodegradable insecticide, which is fat soluble but insoluble in water. It persists in the environment for a very long period. Being fat soluble, it accumulates in the animal tissues and gets concentrated at different trophic levels of food chain. In each step, DDT is more concentrated — this is called biomagnification.

question: Green-house effect refers to
question_latex: Green-house effect refers to
solution: Green house effect is the warming up of earth due to accumulation of green house gases. Green house gases mainly include carbon dioxide ($CO_2$), methane ($CH_4$), chlorofluorocarbons ($CFCs$), etc.

question: Foul smell in the water bodies of tanks, ponds, etc., is due to
question_latex: Foul smell in the water bodies of tanks, ponds, etc., is due to
solution: Eutrophication is natural state in many lakes and ponds, which have a rich supply of nutrients. This leads to decomposition of nutrients through bacteria and other decomposers by the process of anaerobic respiration. This causes foul smell.

question: Heating up of earth's atmosphere occurs due to _____ from earth's surface. [MHT-CET 2019]
question_latex: Heating up of earth's atmosphere occurs due to \underline{\hspace{1cm}} from earth's surface. [MHT-CET 2019]
solution: Heating up of earth's atmosphere occurs due to trapped infrared rays reflected from earth's surface. The greenhouse gases trap the heat radiating from the earth's surface.

question: The percentage of forest cover recommended by the national forest policy (1988) is
question_latex: The percentage of forest cover recommended by the national forest policy (1988) is
options: A. 33% for plains and 67% for hills
B. 37% for plains and 63% for hills
C. 20% for plains and 70% for hills
D. 23% for plains and 77% for hills
answer: A. 33% for plains and 67% for hills
solution: The National Forest Policy (1988) aims at increasing forest cover of the country both in plains and hills. The percentage of forest cover recommended by the National Forest Policy (1988) is 33% for plains and 67% for hills.

question: The term ........ is used for category of species that possesses high risk of extinction with very few surviving members. [2022]
question_latex: The term ........ is used for category of species that possesses high risk of extinction with very few surviving members. [2022]
solution: The term 'critically endangered' is used for category of species that possesses high risk of extinction with very few surviving members.

question: Action of UV rays on ______ forms ozone layer.
question_latex: Action of UV rays on \underline{\hspace{1cm}} forms ozone layer.
solution: Action of UV rays on Oxygen ($O_2$) forms ozone layer situated in stratosphere as about 5 km thick blanket. $CFC$ on reacting with UV will degrade ozone.

question: Ozone hole results in
question_latex: Ozone hole results in
options: A. UV radiation reaches the earth
solution: Ozone is formed in the stratosphere by UV-radiation. Ozone layer of stratosphere protects the earth's living organisms from UV rays (less than 300 nm). Depletion or thinning of ozone layer allows harmful UV rays to reach earth and causes skin cancer, cataract, etc.

question: The water hyacinth (Eichhornia crassipes) is a native of ______ [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The water hyacinth (Eichhornia crassipes) is a native of \underline{\hspace{1cm}} [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Water hyacinth (Eichhornia crassipes) is an aquatic plant, native of Amazon basin, highly problematic invasive species. It was first introduced in India for its lovely purple coloured flowers. But now it is a nuisance as it grows excessively and covers entire water body. It is commonly called 'Terror of Bengal'.

question: In India, hot spot area is found in
question_latex: In India, hot spot area is found in
solution: Out of the 25 hotspots of the world, two are found in India. These are Western Ghats and Eastern Himalayas and these extend to the neighbouring countries also. These areas show high degree of endemism and are inhabited by a wide variety of flowering plants, swallow-tailed butterflies, amphibians, reptiles and mammals.

question: Recent reports of acid rains in big industrial cities are due to the effect of atmospheric pollution by
question_latex: Recent reports of acid rains in big industrial cities are due to the effect of atmospheric pollution by
options: A. More release of NO2 and SO2 by burning of fossil fuels
B. More release of CO2 by burning of coal/wood cutting of forests and increasing populations
C. Excessive release of NH3 by coal gas/industries
D. Excessive release of CO by incomplete combustion of carbonaceous fuels
answer: A. More release of NO2 and SO2 by burning of fossil fuels
solution: The acid rain is the cocktail of $H_2SO_4$ and $HNO_3$. The $SO_2$ and $NO_2$ produced during the combustion of coal and petroleum reacts with water vapour and forms $H_2SO_4$ and $HNO_3$ respectively.

question: Taj Mahal marble is affected by
question_latex: Taj Mahal marble is affected by
solution: $SO_2$ emitted from Mathura refinery (located about 40 km from Taj Mahal) as well as from foundries, power houses and railway yards get mixed with the atmospheric moisture and get converted into sulphuric acid, which settle down on the exterior of Taj Mahal. It reacts with marble ($CaCO_3$) leading to corrosion and discolouration of the monument.

question:
Given below are two statements. [MHT-CET 2024]
Statement-I: Rauwolfia vomitoria found in Himalayan ranges shows variation in potency and concentration of active component reserpine.
Statement-II: Ecosystem diversity is observed in regions of Rann of Kutch.
In the light of above statement, choose the correct option.
question_latex:
Given below are two statements. [MHT-CET 2024]
Statement-I: Rauwolfia vomitoria found in Himalayan ranges shows variation in potency and concentration of active component reserpine.
Statement-II: Ecosystem diversity is observed in regions of Rann of Kutch.
In the light of above statement, choose the correct option.
options: A. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
B. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
C. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
D. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
answer: C. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
solution: Ecosystem diversity is not observed in regions of Rann of Kutch. Statement I is correct as Rauwolfia vomitoria found in Himalayan ranges does show variation in potency and concentration of active component reserpine.

question: Nutrient enrichment of a lake will cause
question_latex: Nutrient enrichment of a lake will cause
solution: Eutrophication is the increase in amount of nutrients in water due to detergents, pesticides, etc., and it leads to organic loading, depletion of $O_2$, etc.

question: Which one of the following is non-renewable exhaustible natural resource?
question_latex: Which one of the following is non-renewable exhaustible natural resource?
solution: Minerals and fossil fuels are the non-renewable (cannot be regenerated after being used up) and exhaustible (limited) resources, while water, wildlife, soil fertility and aquatic plants and animals all are renewable resources.

question: The dolphin found in Chilka lake is
question_latex: The dolphin found in Chilka lake is
solution: The Irrawady dolphin (Orcaella brevirotris) is the flagship species of Chilka lake. Chilka is home to the only known population of Irrawady dolphins of India and one of only two lagoons in the world that are home to this species.
